The three models in the lot, there's a picture of them stuck up there, but you know, there's six movie tickets stuck up there and there's an Arizona baseball cap stuck up there. So I remember she had a meeting one time, she hosted a meeting for the Arizona Card Practice Association. And most of these guys were struck, her practice movement by the way, but mostly. So they came to her office. And most were appalled and one was vocally and loudly appalled at how unprofessional and unseemly. I mean it's the guys that, well in my treatment groups, I have reproductions of Monet paintings, gorgeous frames. And the answer to that guy is yeah, but I had 36 referrals last month. So there's a lot of that that really is not law. So I hear it a lot and it's very rarely true. Just simple things too, like one of the things is, and you don't do it, I don't think maybe you do directly for a referral, but anyone can do is the simple thank you note. You say you answer all of your white mail and I think that helps start referrals too. Absolutely. So tell them, first of all, explain what white mail is and then why, then what you do. So. Because anyone, it doesn't matter when you can do this, you can use this. So part of a referral machine is its foundation that it sits on. So if you get a generator for your house, which I recommend, if you get a generator for your house, but you don't put a concrete pad down for it and you just stick it out there on the hill, it will not function very well. So the foundation underneath the referral machine is at least as important as the machine. So the minimum parts of the foundation are you got to do good work, all that. Then there's a relationship layer on top of that. And there's sort of semi-mechanized parts of that, customer newsletter. There's the responsiveness to people who communicate with you or do things for you. And so it feeds upon itself, by the way. So white mail, leaping to that. White mail is a direct marketing term for anything that comes in a mail that doesn't have a customer service issue in it or an order. So these are complaints. These are questions. These are success reports. These are thank yous. These are, I wanted to let you know, I told Billy Bob about you. You should be hearing from Billy Bob. This is what this stuff is. So depending upon the category of business you're in, you'll get more or less of it. It'll be more personal or less personal. The nature of what I do, a lot of it is lengthy and a lot of it is somewhat personal. But some of it is very, you know, run of the mill stuff. I have always and continue to do so answered all the white mail. That could be as simple as a at a boy, Dan, faxes back to Bill. Or it could be a two page letter answering something Bill has raised. Bill, hey, I wanted to tell you I told so and so about you. You may be calling. Now I'm going to recognize that whether that guy ever calls me or not. Because he went above and beyond. And even if he's ineffective at it, if he does it eight and 12 times, one of them's going to come my way. If he only does it once and never does it again, well, the odds aren't in my favor. So I got to reward each step of compliant, you know, behavior. And we don't, we just don't thank people enough for the things they do for us over, above and beyond giving us money in exchange for goods and services. So so far we've learned about how to the importance of referrals, the hidden benefits of referrals, why the business owner, the entrepreneur doesn't ask for referrals. We talked about why the customer just doesn't refer. Even what to do if you're in a business that you can't reward referrals.
